summary: This brief post announces O'Neil's intention to combine two topics she's been meaning to write about: the Carmen Segarra whistleblower recordings from the NY Fed and the fairness of workplace personality tests. She creates a satirical 'mash-up' imagining what personality test Segarra might have faced when applying for her job at the Federal Reserve. The post is essentially a teaser linking to a satirical Google Forms personality test O'Neil created.
key_points: The post references Carmen Segarra's leaked recordings exposing regulatory capture at the NY Fed
O'Neil connects concerns about workplace personality tests to the broader culture of regulatory agencies
The satirical test format suggests personality screening may filter out exactly the kind of independent thinkers regulators need
money_quotes: I've decided to do a mash-up just for fun, imagining what Carmen had to go through to get her job.
key_insight: The culture and hiring practices of financial regulators may systematically select against the independent-minded people needed to actually hold banks accountable.
